lede: A look at April 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Émile Zola and Serge Gainsbourg, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Garissa University College attack
events.desc: Gunmen attack Garissa University College in Kenya, killing at least 148 people and wounding 79 others.

events.title: Hatton Garden safe deposit burglary
events.desc: Four men steal items worth up to £200 million from an underground safe deposit facility in London's Hatton Garden area in what has been called the "largest burglary in English legal history."

events.title: 2012 Oikos University shooting
events.desc: A mass shooting at Oikos University in California leaves seven people dead and three injured.

events.title: Israel
events.desc: Israeli forces surround the Church of the Nativity in Bethlehem, into which armed Palestinians had retreated.

events.title: New York (state)
events.desc: In New York, Mafia boss John Gotti is convicted of murder and racketeering and is later sentenced to life in prison.

events.title: Alabama
events.desc: Alabama governor George Wallace, a former segregationist, best known for the "Stand in the Schoolhouse Door", announces that he will not seek a fifth four-year term and will retire from public life upon the end of his term in January 1987.

events.title: Australian Bureau of Statistics
events.desc: The Australian Bureau of Statistics conducts the country's first national census.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The largest in a series of Southern bread riots occurs in Richmond, Virginia.

events.title: Ludwig van Beethoven
events.desc: Ludwig van Beethoven leads the premiere of his First Symphony in Vienna.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach's cantata Bleib bei uns, denn es will Abend werden, BWV 6, is first performed in Leipzig on Easter Monday.

births.name: Hans Christian Andersen
births.line: Danish novelist, short story writer, and poet

deaths.text: Manoel de Oliveira, Portuguese actor, director, producer, and screenwriter
Pope John Paul II, Head of the Catholic Church from 1978 to 2005
Hannes Alfvén, Swedish physicist and engineer, Nobel Prize laureate
Georges Pompidou, French banker and politician, 19th President of France
Ferdinand I of Aragon, king of Aragon
Baldwin I of Jerusalem, king of Jerusalem

observances.text: Christian feast day: Bronach of Glen-Seichis (Irish martyrology)
Christian feast day: Henry Budd (Anglican Church of Canada)
